meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Solve Deployment Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use this SaaS starter kit framework with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship production-ready apps faster as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: Deployment paralysis usually stems from complex infrastructure overhead. By using a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you abstract away the DevOps layer, allowing you to ship production-ready code instantly. Leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for automated implementation, you eliminate manual configuration errors and ensure consistent, scalable deployments every time.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, the client deadline is looming, and your production environment is throwing cryptic 500 errors. You've built a beautiful feature, but the deployment pipeline has become a graveyard of configuration files and broken environment variables. The promise of 'shipping fast' feels like a distant fantasy when you spend 80% of your time wrestling with infrastructure.

It is time to stop reinventing the wheel. Professional-grade SaaS starter kits act as your force multiplier, codifying best practices into a repeatable blueprint. By integrating the structural precision of Antigravity with the autonomous execution of Claude Code, you transform from a struggling coder into an elite builder who ships reliable software on command.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Select a robust SaaS boilerplate that includes authentication, billing, and database ORM pre-wired. This prevents the 'blank canvas' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project structure for a SaaS starter kit using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include folder structures for modular API routes and shared UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure based on the Antigravity architecture spec.'

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Deployment failures are often caused by out-of-sync database schemas. Automate migrations to ensure local and production environments match perfectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a migration script that safely syncs local Supabase schema changes to the production instance without downtime.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the migration scripts and add a pre-deployment check to ensure schema parity.'

steps.title: Configure Environment Orchestration
steps.description: Stop using .env files manually. Use an orchestration pattern that validates environment variables before the build process begins.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Zod-based environment variable validator to ensure all required keys are present before build execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add environment variable validation and inject secrets via CLI config.'

steps.title: Implement CI/CD Deployment Hooks
steps.description: Connect your repository to an automated pipeline that triggers on push, ensuring your code is tested and deployed without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a GitHub Actions workflow that runs automated testing and deploys the application to Vercel upon successful merge to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the CI/CD pipeline configuration file for automated Vercel deployment.'

steps.title: Stress Test and Ship
steps.description: Run a final simulation of the deployment process to identify potential bottlenecks before the client sees the project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist of deployment health checks including response latency, auth redirects, and database connection pooling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Execute the final deployment health check script and report any potential points of failure.'

pro_tips: Always use a 'Feature Flag' system even in starter kits to decouple code deployment from feature release.
Store your infrastructure-as-code in the same repo as your application logic to maintain a single source of truth.
Implement 'Health Endpoints' that your deployment provider can ping to verify the application is ready to accept traffic.
Use ephemeral preview environments for every pull request to catch deployment bugs before they hit main.
Automate your database backups to S3 as part of the deployment lifecycle to ensure zero-data-loss recovery.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, audit your build logs for latency, then move on to adding custom middleware for advanced authentication flows.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the infrastructure and start shipping the features that pay the bills. With this workflow, you can cut your deployment time by 90% and focus on scaling your business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
